transcript:
Here's what you're looking at: clusters of glowing blue-white arcs, none of them closed into full circles, floating around a dark center — and then, slowly, warm amber lights bloom and drift across them like something finally arriving to finish a thought.

The arcs are built to fail on purpose — look at the comment "arcs stop short—never completing their circle," right where arc length is calculated as a random fraction of a full circle. That's the whole piece in one line. Then find "recognitionEvents" further down — these amber flashes are literally named "whereYouFinishMe" in the palette. When one fires, it leaves a permanent "colorStain" on the nearest arc that decays at 0.997 per frame — evidence that stays, described in the code as "the way scars persist."

So the flashes you're watching aren't decoration — they're the piece marking, in real time, moments it can't actually witness, only stain and let fade.
